MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Oct-10-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT UNION CARBIDE CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.
APPEARANCES: David L. Amell, Esq. of Paul, Hanley & Harley LLP 510/559-9980 x135 for pltf & opposing party; Gerard P. Fox, Esq. 310/500-3578, Kim Zeldin, Esq. 415/489-7755, & Raul Perez, Esq. 310/500-3627 of Liner Yankelevitz Sunshine & Regenstreif LLP for deft & moving party.
RULING: Defendant and moving party did not comply with the tentative ruling rule (re giving proper notice to opposing party of their intent to appear at the hearing). Therefore, the Court adopted its tentative ruling - denied. Defendant failed to shift the burden of proof. There is more than sufficient objective evidence that plaintiff was exposed to asbestos in Hamilton products. The bulk supplier doctrine is inapplicable. Jenkins vs. T & N PLC (1996) 45 Cal. App. 4th 1224. Defendant failed to shift burden of proof on the issue of punitive damages by failing to address special interrogatory responses 25, 26 and 27. Motion for Summary Adjudication is granted as to intentional tort and false representation claim. There is a lack of reliance on plaintiff's part. Order signed in open court. (302/PJM/AY) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600
